behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module handoff (manifest parsing/resolution -> dependency graph -> vulnerability-check input), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real external API/OS contract verification. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System was considered, but disqualified because the behavior does not explicitly require real external response contract or external side-effect readback.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against fixture projects (e.g., npm with lockfile, pip with pinned transitive deps) and assert the emitted dependency list sent to the vulnerability-check stage includes the full direct+transitive set with correct names/versions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD trigger: explicit need to verify real external query/response contract for version-range matching. MOCK-HIDE: a stub can always return correctly filtered CVEs and hide wrong query parameterization or provider-side semantics. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration was rejected because behavior text targets real query correctness by version range, which requires external contract validation.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability API (or sanctioned test endpoint), query packages with multiple known vulnerable/non-vulnerable ranges and verify returned CVEs match the manifest’s exact version constraints, not package-name-only matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering logic on in-memory data; no module interaction or external boundary required beyond existing unit scope. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted CVE list with mixed severities and CVSS ties into the sorter and assert exact expected order (severity rank first, CVSS descending within same severity).

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on parser/editor/serializer interaction and state preservation, not only one function. REAL-INFRA: local file round-trip is sufficient; no explicit external contract. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require external API/subprocess contract or externally visible side-effect beyond local artifact integrity.
behaviors.test_description: Perform end-to-end manifest update through the real edit pipeline (parse -> patch -> write/readback) and assert a single dependency version changed while all other entries (including resolved dependency blocks) are byte- or structure-equivalent as appropriate.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD trigger 3: must prove externally visible side effect via round-trip readback on real repository (PR exists + diff content). MOCK-HIDE: mocked git/provider clients can hide auth, branch, API, and diff publication failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against a real test repo and real PR provider integration, then read back the created PR and verify it exists, targets expected branch/base, and contains exactly one dependency-version change in the diff.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic semver comparison logic at function/object boundary; no extra reality signal from higher-level checks is required for this behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Assert semver comparator marks x.y.z -> (x+1).0.0 as breaking and non-major increments as non-breaking across normal and edge-case version strings.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff (semver decision -> PR module gating -> reporting output). REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require proving skip against a real external provider, only correct internal gating plus surfaced flag. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System considered but disqualified because no explicit requirement to validate external API contract or round-trip external side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner flow with a fixture vulnerability requiring a major bump, using wired internal modules and a spy/fake PR client; assert PR creation path is not invoked and scanner output includes the breaking-change review flag.
